data.title: 5 Top Stocks For The Global Rotation
data.description:
International Stocks Outperforming U.S. Equities

As geopolitical tensions present investors with a challenging environment, diversification across sectors and assets can help stabilize portfolios for the long run, in addition to broader geographic exposure. International stocks offer a solid opportunity to spread risk as U.S. stocks underperform while trading at historically elevated valuations.

Around 30 country ETFs have outperformed the S&P 500 in the past year across multiple regions, signaling broad geographic participation, with gains powered by key structural tailwinds and sector trends. Metals, energy, and mining stocks have driven strong performance in Latin America and South Africa, while tech, industrial, and financial stocks have supported gains in Asia and Europe.

Heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have added volatility to global commodity markets, particularly for metals and energy stocks. Prices for these resources can move sharply in response to supply disruptions, shifting expectations around global growth, and changes in investor sentiment. This is why it's important that investors interested in gaining exposure to these markets ensure stocks have solid underlying fundamentals.

How I Chose Top Global Stocks

Using the Seeking Alpha Stock Screener, I filtered for Strong Buy stocks outside the United States but focused on geographic diversity, pulling together a basket of five companies with operations spanning Latin America, Asia, Europe, Africa, and Canada. The basket includes stocks in the metal mining and energy sectors, which have experienced significant volatility. Investors should assess their risk tolerance, as these types of investments are not for everyone - share prices can swing dramatically based on factors beyond each company’s control.

Seeking Alpha Quant Ratings are generated by a proprietary model that analyzes more than 100 metrics for each stock relative to sector peers and grades them across five factors: Growth, Value, Profitability, Earnings Revisions, and Momentum.

1. Nexa Resources S.A. (NEXA)

  • Market Capitalization: $1.51B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Materials

  • Industry: Diversified Metals and Mining

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/6/2026): 10 out of 279

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/6/2026): 2 out of 60

A new member of the PRO Quant Portfolio, Nexa is a major zinc producer, accounting for about 4% of global production, with mining and smelting operations across Brazil and Peru. NEXA commands a dominant market position, accounting for more than 80% of the Latin American zinc metal market, enabling it to sell at premium prices and maintain a competitive edge in cost efficiency.

With vertically integrated operations, Nexa is well positioned to exploit structural demand tailwinds driven by the global energy transition, with zinc increasingly listed as a critical mineral by the world’s largest economies. The stock has been hit in the Iran war selloff, but is now trading at an even more attractive valuation, and is likely to capitalize on rising commodity prices.

NEXA vs. S&P 500 Materials Sector (XLB): 1Y Price Return

Structural concentrate tightness and resilient demand supported zinc prices, driving Nexa’s annual revenue up 9% to $3B in FY 2025. Strong prices, operating leverage, execution, and byproduct contribution helped push Q4 margins to 33% vs. 27% in Q4 2024. Although annual zinc concentrate production slipped by 3% YoY to 316Kt, due to operational constraints impacting grades and volumes earlier in the year, output bounced back soundly, up 9% YoY in the fourth quarter. Nexa’s production profile also offers exposure to rising base and precious metal prices - in 2025, it produced 11M pounds of silver and 33Kt of copper.

Looking ahead, Nexa’s midpoint guidance for zinc production is at ~335Ktpa for 2026, while the company expects tight inventories and rising demand to support metal price levels. Backed by bullish earnings revisions, Nexa’s EPS is expected to soar by 122% in FY 2026, according to consensus estimates. Strong return on equity and expanding EBITDA margins support Nexa’s ‘B’ Profitability Grade.

Despite exceptional momentum, Nexa trades at a mere 6x earnings vs. the sector’s 16x, while price/sales and price/cash flow multiples are at significant discounts to the sector. A high-momentum Latin American metal mining stock, supported by powerful industry tailwinds, offers investors solid international exposure.

2. China Yuchai International Limited (CYD)

  • Market Capitalization: $1.53B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Industrials

  • Industry: Construction Machinery & Heavy Transportation Equipment

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/6/2026): 18 out of 611

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/6/2026): 1 out of 28

Another PQP stock and leading powertrain solutions provider, China Yuchai International, specializes in building engines for trucks, buses, construction equipment, and marine applications. CYD has a strong foothold in the Chinese market but is deepening exposure across ASEAN. CYD has been developing hydrogen and methanol combustion engines while launching new high-horsepower products to meet booming data center demand.

CYD vs. S&P 500 Industrial Sector (XLI): 1Y Price Return

Better-than-expected truck and bus engine sales, which significantly outpaced the market, drove revenue up 28% to $3.5B in FY 2025, while operating profit surged 82.7% to $155.2M, supported by continuing cost reduction initiatives. Sales to data centers increased to 2,000 units vs. 750 in the prior year as surging AI workloads required stronger power-generation solutions. CYD’s ability to expand globally and plans to grow its reach even further support a strong earnings outlook in the next three years.

"In addition to our continued expansion in China, we enhanced our footprint in overseas markets with a strategic agreement in Vietnam, shipped high–quality castings to Germany, and delivered buses powered by Yuchai natural gas engines in Mexico. New partnerships with additional global industrial leaders will further strengthen market access in the future,” President Weng Ming Hoh said in a press release.

Strong financial results contributed to a top-notch Growth Grade, driven by a 44%-plus forward EPS long-term growth rate (3-5Y CAGR) and surging cash flow, operating profit, and return on equity.

CYD’s pullback as the Middle East conflict disrupts global supply chains offers investors an opportunity. A PEG of 0.32, representing an 82% discount to the sector, underpins a ‘A’ Valuation Grade, along with a P/E of 14x. Well positioned for more growth in the high-power generation market, while trading at an attractive price, makes CYD a strong pick for an international portfolio.

3. Centerra Gold Inc. (CGAU)

  • Market Capitalization: $3.68B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Materials

  • Industry: Gold

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/6/2026): 14 out of 279

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/6/2026): 6 out of 49

Added to the PQP portfolio last month, CGAU offers a potential buy-the-dip opportunity after the latest slide in gold. The company is focused on mining gold and copper primarily in North America, Turkey, and other international locations. CGAU has benefited from recent permit approvals to extend operations at its Mount Milligan mine in British Columbia through 2035 as it advances plans to extend the mine life to 2045.

CGAU vs. Gold Mining Peers (GDX): 1Y Price Return

Beating top- and bottom-line expectations for Q4 2025, strong operational performance at Mount Milligan and Oksut, in addition to elevated metal prices, drove revenue up 33% YoY to $401.6M, boosted cash flow, and expanded EBITDA margins. For the full-year, CGAU produced 275.3Koz of gold and 50.4Mlbs of copper, and outperformed cost guidance while investing in a robust pipeline of growth projects.

Centerra also advanced studies of the Kemess project in British Columbia, which has the potential to significantly lift the company’s production profile. Kemess has a 15-year mine life with average annual production of 171Koz of gold and 61Mlbs of copper and, according to the preliminary economic assessment (PEA), is valued at $1.1B based on after-tax NPV at 5%.

The company’s robust margins and strong return on equity underpin a high-quality Profitability Grade. The profitable growth is expected to continue based on strong forward EBITDA and earnings growth metrics, and nine upward earnings revisions to one downward revision in the last 90 days.

CGAU is trading at 10x earnings, while its PEG TTM is at a 96% discount to the sector. Leveraged to gold and copper prices with significant growth plans, CGAU is a high-performing gold stock worth keeping an eye on for a commodity price rebound.

4. Neste Oyj (NTOIY)

  • Market Capitalization: $21.96B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Energy

  • Industry: Oil and Gas Refining and Marketing

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/6/2026): 4 out of 224

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/6/2026): 1 out of 18

Finland-headquartered Neste Oyj, a PQP stock, is a prominent player in sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and renewable diesel, with a network of around 1,000 fueling stations, benefiting from regulatory tailwinds and strategic capacity expansions. Beyond SAF and renewable diesel, the company is also expanding into renewable feedstocks for plastics and chemicals.

NTOIY vs. S&P 500 Energy Sector (XLE): 1Y Price Return

After a rough start in 2025 amid depressed oil and renewable product markets, Neste implemented stricter capital discipline and performance improvement measures that helped boost operational efficiency. Full-year 2025 revenue came in at $22.34B, while adjusted EBITDA rose significantly as higher sales volumes and margins offset lower prices, with strong performance across renewable and oil products, supported by improved utilization.

Long term, the company estimates renewable production capacity will reach 6.8M tonnes in 2027. Neste’s A+ Growth Grade is driven by an EPS forward long-term growth rate of +90% and surging operating profits and cash flow. Meanwhile, positive analyst revisions reflect optimism in its earnings outlook for FY 2026.

Neste offers an attractive valuation with a PEG that's at a 78% discount to the sector. A leader in renewable energy that's riding industry megatrends, Neste's excellent momentum and long-term earnings growth prospects continue to drive a Strong Buy rating.

5. Sibanye Stillwater Limited (SBSW)

  • Market Capitalization: $10.12B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Sector: Materials

  • Industry: Precious Metals and Minerals

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/6/2026): 7 out of 279

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/6/2026): 1 out of 7

A PQP member since January, Sibanye Stillwater has footholds in both traditional and green economies, offering exposure to precious metals with long-term lithium optionality. After dipping hard in the precious metals pullback, SBSW is trading at a steep discount to the sector, while facing a potential catalyst if the U.S. imposes tariffs on palladium - a decision expected by mid 2026.

SBSW vs. Platinum (PPLT) and Gold (GLD) Prices: 1Y Return

SBSW has core mining assets strategically positioned in South Africa’s Bushveld Complex, which holds the world’s largest reserves of platinum group metals. Sibanye’s South African PGM and gold operations drove just more than 70% of group revenue in FY 2025, followed by U.S. PGM and recycling operations (~20%), with the remainder from zinc, nickel, and other metal products.

SBSW reported $7.25B in annual revenue in 2025, up from $6.1B in 2024, while adjusted EBITDA grew by 189% YoY to $2.1B. The results were driven largely by higher realized precious metal prices and strength in the recycling business, boosted by the acquisition of Metallix in North Carolina. In terms of long-term outlook, construction on the Keliber lithium project in Finland is expected to be completed in Q1 2026. Nearly 80% owned by SBSW, Keliber has an 18-year mine life and 248Kt of lithium reserves.

Surging revenue, EBITDA, and cash flow growth in the past 12 months drive Sibanye's A+ Growth Grade. The stock is trading at 6x earnings - a 60% discount to the sector, and attractive EV/EBITDA and price/cash flow underpin an ‘A’ Valuation Grade. Also supporting the valuation is Sibanye’s first annual dividend in three years. SBSW paid out 35% of normalized earnings for a dividend yielding around 2%.

Leveraged to PGM and gold prices, with growing exposure to the green economy, SBSW is one of the top diversified mining Strong Buys. Sibanye Stillwater wraps up my basket of global stocks that offer investors geographic diversification in volatile times.

Top Global Stocks for a Rotation from U.S. Equities

International stocks have outperformed U.S. equities, with 30 country ETFs topping the S&P 500 in the past year. The performance comes as U.S. stocks look expensive relative to historical averages. Investors faced with a volatile environment may want to consider diversification strategies across assets and regions. Broad geographic exposure provides investors an opportunity to rotate away from a U.S.-heavy portfolio and capture opportunities emerging across global markets. SA Quant identified five Strong Buy stocks spanning Asia, Latin America, Europe, Africa, and Canada, with strong fundamentals and attractive valuations.

We have many stocks with strong buy recommendations, and you can filter them using Stock Screens to suit your specific investment objectives. Alternatively, Alpha Picks might be ideal if you're interested in two monthly stock picks of the top "strong buy" Quant stocks. Seeking Alpha’s quant ratings and investment research tools help to ensure you are furnished with the best resources to make informed investment decisions while taking the emotion out of investing.

If you’re looking for more frequent investment ideas, we launched the PRO Quant Portfolio in June. It’s a weekly rebalanced selection of our Top 30 Quant Strong Buy stocks, spanning multiple regions and market caps. Happy investing!

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.
